rag_rules: Preserve owner scope: place type-level contracts only where referenced features are visible at that component scope.
Keep implementation-level content in implementation scope when it depends on internal structure or bindings.
Do not introduce references across scopes that are not visible from the chosen owner.
generic_rules: Insert new annex content without changing existing component structure, subcomponents, connections, features, or properties.
Preserve architecture text outside the annex and keep edits minimal and owner-local.
If a statement depends only on component-visible ports, prefer the component owner scope rather than internal paths.
If a statement requires implementation-only references, keep it confined to the implementation owner scope.
syntax_or_pattern_reminders: AADL annex blocks must remain attached to the correct owning component or implementation and use valid annex delimiters.
Avoid subcomponent port paths in a higher-level owner unless those names are explicitly visible in that scope.
anti_patterns: Do not move or rewrite architectural declarations just to add behavioral text.
Do not place implementation-only references into a component-level owner scope.
Do not create cross-owner references that violate visibility rules.
irrelevant_topics: Validator-specific type repair details for bool/int/real declarations
